curbcheck · reading diagnostic · n=8 hard samples

Where the read breaks, and what recovers it.

A component-level failure analysis on the hardest, most faded SF parking signs. For each sample we trace three reading components, raw OCR, OCR under the best image transform, and the VLM, then mark the exact point where the read is recovered. The question is not "is this sign hard," it is "which component carries it."

5
OCR solves
1
Transform rescues
1
Needs VLM
1
Flag / illegible
Lead finding

Of 8 "hard" samples, 7 are recoverable: 5 by raw OCR, 1 by a CLAHE-style transform, 1 by the VLM. Only 1 carries a genuinely illegible field worth filtering out. Most hard signs are legible; the difficulty was the reading component, not the sign.

Action, build the three-part stack:

OCR-assist → VLM+ CLAHE preprocesslow-legibility flag

Feed the image and the OCR text to the VLM, add a CLAHE contrast pass ahead of OCR, and flag the tiny illegible tail rather than guessing.

Pipeline traceread each card left→right
pass · full read partial · some fields fail · garbled / empty recovers first component that fully reads it
5

OCR already reads it

Legible signs. Raw Apple Vision OCR extracts the rule directly. The VLM stumbled on density or nth-week nuance, not legibility; feed it the OCR text and it recovers.

SOLVED
SAMPLE 01 SOLVED
sf_12563307readable
Ground truth

2 HOUR PARKING 8AM-4PM, 2nd & 4th TUESDAY of month (time_limit, weeks 2,4)

Raw OCR
14 tokens, full read
→
+ Clahe
clahe confirms
→
VLM
OCR-assist resolves it
Raw OCR (original) 2w and 40 TUESDAY | OF THE MONTH | 2 HOUR | PARKING | 8A.M. TO 4 P.M.

Dx Legible; raw OCR nails it including the week-of-month line. The VLM's difficulty here was the nth-week nuance, which OCR text hands over directly.

Raw OCR (original) NG | 2/26 | NO STOPPING | 12/22/25 - 06/22/26 | 24 hours | - Sun | Stret Use | NO STOPPING | 12/22/25 - 06/22/26 | TAM - 6PM | Mon - Sat | 1303 LARKIN ST | on LARGN ST. PINE ST - CALIFORNIA ST (137 1t) - West | D+H Construction | (707) 290-8610 | Permit 8 25-59-280490 | Street Use | NO STOPPING | 12/22/25 - 06/22/26 | 24 hours | - Sun | 1303 LARKIN ST | on LARKIN STIPINE ST - CALIFORNIA ST (137 t) - West | D+H Construction | (707) 290-8610 | Permit & 25-59-293490 | Street Use

Dx Dense / multi-sign; OCR reads it cleanly. Hard for the VLM (it conflates signs on busy poles), easy once OCR lists the text.

Dx Sharp; OCR perfect.

1

A CLAHE transform rescues it

Raw OCR returns nothing. A contrast-limited adaptive histogram equalization pass plus upscale recovers the full read. The clearest argument for a preprocessing stage.

RESCUED
SAMPLE 06 RESCUED
oak_17919312readable (small/distant)
Ground truth

NO PARKING 9AM-12NOON, 1st & 3rd MONDAYS (no_parking, weeks 1,3)

Raw OCR
0 tokens, empty
→
+ Clahe
clahe: 9 tokens, full read
→
VLM
not required
Raw OCR (original) ∅ empty; no text extracted

Dx Raw OCR returns NOTHING; CLAHE contrast + upscale recovers the full sign. The clearest win for image preprocessing.

1

Needs the VLM

Legible to a human, but OCR garbles it under every transform. The text is present; OCR cannot extract it. This is the case the vision model must carry.

VLM-ONLY
SAMPLE 07 VLM-ONLY
sf_13900373readable (faded but legible)
Ground truth

2 HOUR PARKING 9AM-4PM, MON THRU FRI, EXCEPT AREA O PERMIT (permit_limit)

Raw OCR
9 tokens, garbled
→
+ Upscale2X
all transforms garble
→
VLM
human / VLM reads it
Raw OCR (original) H06R | PARING | 9 KM. 14 | MON CR | PERNITS | MEN

Dx Faded permit sign. Raw OCR and every transform garble it; a human/strong VLM reads it. The info is present, OCR cannot extract it.

1

Flag and filter out

A genuinely illegible field that no component recovers. Part of the sign is readable; one field is unreadable even by a human. Flag as low-legibility rather than guess.

FLAG
SAMPLE 08 FLAG
oak_17678584partial
Ground truth

NO PARKING ?-6AM (top, start illegible) + NO PARKING 12:30PM-? THURSDAYS

Raw OCR
1 field only
→
+ Upscale2X
upscale2x: bottom sign only
→
VLM
top field unreadable
Raw OCR (original) NO | PARKING, | ВИ 19 6 AM

Dx Two stacked faded/graffitied signs. Bottom (Thursdays 12:30PM) is recoverable; the TOP sign's start time is genuinely unreadable even by a human -> flag as low-legibility.

curbcheck · reading diagnostic · Apple Vision OCR on original + 4 transforms (original, upscale2x, gray_autocontrast, clahe, sharpen) n=8 · SF + Oakland + DPW samples · ground truth = human read
